name: Chang'e 2
description: Began as a higher-resolution remap of the Moon following Chang'e 1, then repurposed for an extended deep-space mission. After completing lunar mapping in 2011, Chang'e 2 departed for Sun-Earth L2 — China's first deep-space mission — and then on to a 2012 flyby of asteroid 4179 Toutatis. Still tracked in heliocentric orbit.
scale_fact: Lunar polar orbit at 100 km altitude during the lunar phase. First Chinese deep-space mission after extended ops; first non-NASA-non-ESA mission to L2.
